results.content:

Lindsey W | 01-29-2025

Had the company come and install, which after the service was available, was quick. Internet speeds started off great, but over the last 6 months it's declined. Constantly dropped connections, slow speeds. I work from home and at least once a day my connection is lost for about 5 to 10 mins during my workday. Incredibly frustrating. We often switch to use our mobile data on our phones since the internet is unstable. We switched from Tmobile's internet and it's about the same, if not worse with connection speeds. Not sure there are good options out there.

THE WORST< WORST experience i have ever had with internet provider. Customer service is a joke. Never get to actually talk to real person. No reliable internet service after paying for 2 months....The answer as to why i don't have internet is always the same. There is an outage in your area. Check the app? NO OUTAGES REPORTED IN MY AREA???? Oh and by the way my next door neighbor, who has quantum, has internet service but im in an outage. After 2 months of spotty internet i had enough. Try to cancel? Took 20 minutes of customer service saying we can credit your account. We can give you discount to make them understand i would go without internet if they were the only providers. HOW ABOUT GIVING ME INTERNET!!.

results.title: quantumfiber.com is rated "Bad" with 1.2 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Looking at 372 reviews, most reviewers were let down by their experience overall. Many customers expressed significant dissatisfaction with the service, citing frequent outages, slow speeds, and unreliable connections that often left them without internet for extended periods. Reviewers also found the customer service to be consistently poor, describing it as inept, unhelpful, and difficult to rea ch, with calls frequently dropping or being handled by staff who were hard to understand. The website and contact methods were also a source of frustration, with non-functional phone numbers and chat boxes that frequently closed, making it nearly impossible to resolve issues. Some people also felt that the installation process was problematic, with technicians failing to show up, arriving late, [...] 5 days ago

Image 38: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## Unreliabl
Got quantum fiber 2 months ago and had a very similar experience to other reviewers on here. We have had 3 outages with 2 lasting 4+ days. Customer service is terrible and does not provide reliable updates. We had Xfinity for 5 years prior and had very few outages and the ones we did have the communication was much better. We will likely switch to ezee fiber or back to Xfinity if our experience doesn't improve. Quantum should spend less money on advertising and door knocking and more money on making a product that doesn't suck.

June 27, 2026

## Avoid ATT and Quantum in Colorado Springs
Moved in to a new home and ordered quantum fiber. Had to take a morning off of work to get an "install". Two days later I'm still waiting for my internet to work. Talked to customer service and they said it will take at least 24 more hours to activate my account. Even worse, they offered to bundle my mobile service with ATT, which offers very limited connectivity. 3 days without a phone or internet. I'm canceling all of it

6/18/26

One star — loyalty means nothing here I’ve been a paying Quantum Fiber customer, on time, every month, keeping the lights on for this company. So it’s beyond frustrating to watch them advertise their best prices exclusively for “new customers” while existing customers like me get left out entirely. Think about that logic: the people who’ve actually been loyal and reliable don’t qualify for the deals… [...] One star — loyalty means nothing here I’ve been a paying Quantum Fiber customer, on time, every month, keeping the lights on for this company. So it’s beyond frustrating to watch them advertise their best prices exclusively for “new customers” while existing customers like me get left out entirely. Think about that logic: the people who’ve actually been loyal and reliable don’t qualify for the deals — but a stranger walking in the door does. That’s backwards. Rewarding new sign-ups while taking your existing base for granted isn’t a strategy, it’s a slap in the face to the customers who got you here. If Quantum Fiber actually valued loyalty, their best available pricing would be offered to everyone, not dangled as bait to chase new accounts. Until that changes, I can’t recommend them. results.title: Why wasn't this game well recieved in the beginning? :: Quantum Break General Discussions
results.content:
Half of those point are ridiculus...its a game, it's made to entertain, not to be realistic.  
  
What's wrong with real actors puting their voices and faces to a character?  
  
Runs badly? Upgrade your pc... I have a CPU from 6 years ago and a 4GB rx470 and it runs flawlesly all maxed...  
  
So either you're a troll or just putting random hating points....  
I mean seriously. I understand some constructive criticism and even want to hear it but your list is just...i can't even find a word for how stupid it is.  
  
I mean...you serioulsy criticize the game for having cutscenes like a TV show?  
Damn son

#3

 is a weird thing to dislike about this game.

results.title: Why I Left Quantum Computing Research
results.content:
2020, I was pretty disappointed by the lack of progress. There were of course, other quantum 
algorithms like for example, phase estimation, which is actually very related to Shor's factoring 
algorithm, and also another one called Hamiltonian simulation, which is quite different and 
allows you to simulate quantum objects. I. Now, five years later, I wanna go back 
and see if, well, there's any new algorithms, but also whether there have been new 
applications found for the existing algorithms. And the spoiler is, I think 
it's been a little bit disappointing, but there have been some hopeful signs. 
Let's start with quantum machine learning. So we've all seen how incredibly good AI 
systems are, and I think there's this worry that sometimes gets brought up in the media [...] and I have no reason not to believe that. I think that we really are on track, but on 
the software front. I think things have been much more disappointing. To explain why I need to 
explain one of the most misunderstood things about quantum computers. Quantum computers are not 
just general purpose super computers. It's not like every single thing that you do on a regular 
computer can be done faster on a quantum computer. Like you can't play games faster on a quantum 
computer. In fact, quantum computers are very specialized at doing very specific tasks, and 
I wanna explain a little bit about quantum computers so you can see why that is. I'm not 
gonna explain what a qubit is or anything like that because if you wanna see a full explanation [...] can't actually be made in practice still. I am actually really hopeful 
about that because usually when there are algorithms of this sort where 
they're sort of relative to some oracle, often people come up with a way to take that 
idea and put it into a practical algorithm. Like I think Shaw's algorithm even came out 
of sort of Oracle like results like that. So that's proof that there really are 
other quantum algorithms out there, and I think it's really exciting. 
If you work in quantum computing, you might have worried that like most of my 
videos sounded quite negative and I'm really sorry about that. I actually don't wanna 
come down too hard on quantum computing. I just think that there has been a bit of
